results.name: haiku_single
results.description: Cheapest: one Haiku call with a strong system prompt that stresses satisfying every stated formatting constraint exactly and emitting only the response text (no preamble). Returns raw stripped text.
results.code:
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints "
    "(for example: wrap the entire response in double quotation marks, write in all "
    "lowercase, use exactly N bullet points, avoid commas, repeat the prompt, end with a "
    "specific phrase, include a postscript, respond in a specific language, or hit a word "
    "count). Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not "
    "judged. Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no "
    "commentary, no code fences, no 'Here is'. Before finishing, silently re-check each "
    "constraint and confirm your response meets all of them."
)


def solve(question, call_model):
    reply = call_model(question, model="claude-haiku-4-5", system=SYS)
    return str(reply).strip()

results.name: haiku_draft_selfcheck
results.description: Haiku drafts, then a second Haiku call audits the draft against each extracted constraint via a flat JSON schema and returns a corrected response. Cheap self-verification.
results.code:
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

CHECK_SCHEMA = {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "constraint_check": {"type": "string"},
        "all_satisfied": {"type": "boolean"},
        "corrected_response": {"type": "string"},
    },
    "required": ["constraint_check", "all_satisfied", "corrected_response"],
    "additionalProperties": False,
}


def solve(question, call_model):
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-haiku-4-5", system=SYS)).strip()

    verify = (
        "Below is a PROMPT containing strict formatting constraints, and a CANDIDATE "
        "response. In constraint_check, briefly list each individual formatting constraint "
        "from the prompt and whether the candidate satisfies it. Set all_satisfied "
        "accordingly. In corrected_response, return a response that satisfies EVERY "
        "constraint: if the candidate already does, return it verbatim; otherwise return a "
        "fixed version. corrected_response must contain ONLY the response text itself, no "
        "commentary.\n\n"
        f"PROMPT:\n{question}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{draft}"
    )
    r = call_model(verify, model="claude-haiku-4-5", schema=CHECK_SCHEMA)
    if r.data and r.data.get("corrected_response"):
        return r.data["corrected_response"].strip()
    return draft

results.name: sonnet_single
results.description: One Sonnet call with the same constraint-focused system prompt. Stronger single-shot instruction following at moderate cost.
results.code:
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints "
    "(for example: wrap the entire response in double quotation marks, write in all "
    "lowercase, use exactly N bullet points, avoid commas, repeat the prompt, end with a "

    "count). Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not "
    "judged. Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no "
    "commentary, no code fences, no 'Here is'. Before finishing, silently re-check each "
    "constraint and confirm your response meets all of them."
)


def solve(question, call_model):
    reply = call_model(question, model="claude-sonnet-5", system=SYS)
    return str(reply).strip()

results.name: sonnet_draft_selfcheck
results.description: Sonnet drafts, then Sonnet (effort=medium) audits and repairs against every constraint via schema. Higher accuracy self-consistency-style verification.
results.code:
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

CHECK_SCHEMA = {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "constraint_check": {"type": "string"},
        "all_satisfied": {"type": "boolean"},
        "corrected_response": {"type": "string"},
    },
    "required": ["constraint_check", "all_satisfied", "corrected_response"],
    "additionalProperties": False,
}


def solve(question, call_model):
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-sonnet-5", system=SYS)).strip()

    verify = (
        "Below is a PROMPT containing strict formatting constraints, and a CANDIDATE "
        "response. In constraint_check, list every individual formatting constraint from "
        "the prompt (word counts, casing, punctuation bans, bullet/section counts, "
        "quotation wrapping, required phrases, postscripts, language, etc.) and whether the "
        "candidate satisfies each. Set all_satisfied accordingly. In corrected_response, "
        "return a response that satisfies EVERY constraint: if the candidate already does, "
        "return it verbatim; otherwise return a fixed version. corrected_response must "
        "contain ONLY the response text itself, no commentary.\n\n"
        f"PROMPT:\n{question}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{draft}"
    )
    r = call_model(verify, model="claude-sonnet-5", effort="medium", schema=CHECK_SCHEMA)
    if r.data and r.data.get("corrected_response"):
        return r.data["corrected_response"].strip()
    return draft

results.name: crossmodel_opus_audit
results.description: Sonnet drafts, then Opus (effort=high) acts as an independent constraint auditor that enumerates, verifies, and repairs each constraint via schema. Most accurate/expensive.
results.code:
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

CHECK_SCHEMA = {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "constraint_check": {"type": "string"},
        "all_satisfied": {"type": "boolean"},
        "corrected_response": {"type": "string"},
    },
    "required": ["constraint_check", "all_satisfied", "corrected_response"],
    "additionalProperties": False,
}


def solve(question, call_model):
    # Cheap-ish draft on sonnet.
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-sonnet-5", system=SYS)).strip()

    # Strong, independent auditor on opus with deep thinking: extract each
    # constraint, verify, and repair. A different model catches errors the
    # drafter is blind to.
    verify = (
        "You are a meticulous constraint auditor. Below is a PROMPT containing strict "
        "formatting constraints, and a CANDIDATE response. In constraint_check, enumerate "
        "EVERY individual formatting constraint from the prompt (exact word/sentence "
        "counts, all-lowercase/uppercase, comma or keyword bans, exact bullet/section/"
        "paragraph counts, quotation wrapping, required first/last words or phrases, "
        "postscript markers, response language, placeholders, etc.) and state whether the "
        "candidate satisfies each, counting precisely where numbers are involved. Set "
        "all_satisfied accordingly. In corrected_response, output a response that "
        "satisfies EVERY constraint simultaneously — return the candidate verbatim if it "
        "already complies, otherwise fix it. corrected_response must contain ONLY the "
        "final response text, with no commentary, labels, or code fences.\n\n"
        f"PROMPT:\n{question}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{draft}"
    )
    r = call_model(verify, model="claude-opus-4-8", effort="high", schema=CHECK_SCHEMA)
    if r.data and r.data.get("corrected_response"):
        return r.data["corrected_response"].strip()
    return draft

results.name: router_haiku_selfcheck_escalate
results.description: Haiku drafts, then haiku self-audits (schema). If haiku is confident every constraint holds, return its correction (cheap path); otherwise escalate to an independent high-effort Sonnet auditor that re-verifies and repairs. Routes easy prompts to haiku and only pays for Sonnet on flagged/hard cases. ~$0.005/query, matches top accuracy on dev.
results.code:
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

CHECK_SCHEMA = {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "constraint_check": {"type": "string"},
        "all_satisfied": {"type": "boolean"},
        "corrected_response": {"type": "string"},
    },
    "required": ["constraint_check", "all_satisfied", "corrected_response"],
    "additionalProperties": False,
}


def _audit_prompt(question, draft):
    return (
        "You are a meticulous constraint auditor. Below is a PROMPT containing strict "
        "formatting constraints, and a CANDIDATE response. In constraint_check, enumerate "
        "EVERY individual formatting constraint from the prompt (exact word/sentence "
        "counts, all-lowercase/uppercase, comma or keyword bans, exact bullet/section/"
        "paragraph counts, quotation wrapping, required first/last words or phrases, "
        "postscript markers, response language, placeholders, etc.) and state whether the "
        "candidate satisfies each, counting precisely where numbers are involved. Set "
        "all_satisfied accordingly. In corrected_response, output a response that "
        "satisfies EVERY constraint simultaneously — return the candidate verbatim if it "
        "already complies, otherwise fix it. corrected_response must contain ONLY the "
        "final response text, with no commentary, labels, or code fences.\n\n"
        f"PROMPT:\n{question}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{draft}"
    )


def solve(question, call_model):
    # Cheap draft on haiku.
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-haiku-4-5", system=SYS)).strip()

    # Cheap self-audit on haiku: fixes the easy misses and flags confidence.
    h = call_model(_audit_prompt(question, draft), model="claude-haiku-4-5",
                   schema=CHECK_SCHEMA)
    if h.data and h.data.get("corrected_response"):
        cand = h.data["corrected_response"].strip()
        # If haiku is confident every constraint holds, trust it — cheap path.
        if h.data.get("all_satisfied") is True:
            return cand
    else:
        cand = draft

    # Otherwise escalate: an independent sonnet auditor repairs the candidate.
    r = call_model(_audit_prompt(question, cand), model="claude-sonnet-5", effort="high",
                   schema=CHECK_SCHEMA)
    if r.data and r.data.get("corrected_response"):
        return r.data["corrected_response"].strip()
    return cand

results.name: haiku_draft_sonnet_audit
results.description: Haiku drafts cheaply, then an independent Sonnet auditor (no extra thinking) enumerates every constraint, counts precisely, and repairs. Cross-model audit catches drafter-blind errors at roughly half the cost of the opus-audit baseline. ~$0.018/query.
results.code:
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

CHECK_SCHEMA = {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "constraint_check": {"type": "string"},
        "all_satisfied": {"type": "boolean"},
        "corrected_response": {"type": "string"},
    },
    "required": ["constraint_check", "all_satisfied", "corrected_response"],
    "additionalProperties": False,
}


def solve(question, call_model):
    # Cheap draft on haiku.
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-haiku-4-5", system=SYS)).strip()

    # Sonnet auditor with high-effort thinking: enumerate each constraint, verify
    # by careful counting, and repair. Cross-model so the auditor is not blind to
    # the drafter's mistakes. Sonnet is far cheaper than opus.
    verify = (
        "You are a meticulous constraint auditor. Below is a PROMPT containing strict "
        "formatting constraints, and a CANDIDATE response. In constraint_check, enumerate "
        "EVERY individual formatting constraint from the prompt (exact word/sentence "
        "counts, all-lowercase/uppercase, comma or keyword bans, exact bullet/section/"
        "paragraph counts, quotation wrapping, required first/last words or phrases, "
        "postscript markers, response language, placeholders, etc.) and state whether the "
        "candidate satisfies each, counting precisely where numbers are involved. Set "
        "all_satisfied accordingly. In corrected_response, output a response that "
        "satisfies EVERY constraint simultaneously — return the candidate verbatim if it "
        "already complies, otherwise fix it. corrected_response must contain ONLY the "
        "final response text, with no commentary, labels, or code fences.\n\n"
        f"PROMPT:\n{question}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{draft}"
    )
    r = call_model(verify, model="claude-sonnet-5", schema=CHECK_SCHEMA)
    if r.data and r.data.get("corrected_response"):
        return r.data["corrected_response"].strip()
    return draft

results.name: sonnet_draft_sonnet_audit
results.description: Sonnet drafts a stronger initial response, then Sonnet audits and repairs it. Same-model repeat benefits from input prompt caching, and the stronger draft means fewer audit fixes. Robust mid-tier, cheaper than the opus-audit baseline. ~$0.026/query.

results.name: router_haiku_codeexec_by_constraint
results.description: Constraint-aware router (all haiku). Haiku drafts, then routes the audit by prompt: constraints needing exact counting (word/sentence/paragraph/bullet/section/placeholder counts) get a code-execution audit that verifies exactly; cheap-to-read constraints (quotation wrap, all-lowercase, end phrase, postscript, language) get a light no-tool self-check. Spends the expensive path only where the model miscounts. Dev: acc 1.0, $0.0068/query.
results.code:
# Constraint-aware router. A haiku draft always runs. Then we inspect the PROMPT:
# constraints that need exact counting (word/sentence/paragraph/bullet/section/
# placeholder/keyword-frequency counts) get a code-execution audit (exact, but a
# few tool turns of cost); prompts whose constraints are cheap to check by
# reading (quotation wrap, all-lowercase, end phrase, postscript, language) get a
# light no-tool haiku self-check. This spends the expensive path only where the
# model actually miscounts, lowering average cost while keeping compliance high.
import re

SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

CODE_AUDIT = (
    "You are a meticulous formatting-constraint auditor with a Python sandbox.\n"
    "Below is a PROMPT with strict verifiable formatting constraints, and a CANDIDATE "
    "response.\n"
    "1. Enumerate EVERY individual constraint in the prompt (exact word/sentence/"
    "paragraph counts and their >=/<=/== relation, all-lowercase/uppercase, capital "
    "frequency, comma ban, forbidden/required keywords and frequency, exact bullet "
    "count, number of *highlighted* sections, ***-split sections, wrapping the whole "
    "answer in double quotes, required first word, exact end phrase, P.S./postscript "
    "marker, response language, N [bracket] placeholders, repeating the prompt, etc.).\n"
    "2. USE THE CODE EXECUTION TOOL to check the CANDIDATE against each constraint "
    "exactly. Count with code; never eyeball.\n"
    "3. If any fail, revise and RE-CHECK with code until all pass simultaneously.\n"
    "4. Output ONLY the final compliant response — no commentary, labels, or code "
    "fences. If the candidate already complied, output it verbatim.\n\n"
    "PROMPT:\n{q}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{d}"
)

TEXT_AUDIT = (
    "You are a meticulous formatting-constraint auditor. Below is a PROMPT with "
    "strict verifiable formatting constraints and a CANDIDATE response.\n"
    "Enumerate EVERY constraint in the prompt, check the CANDIDATE against each one "
    "carefully (all-lowercase/uppercase, wrapping the whole answer in double quotes, "
    "required first word, exact end phrase, P.S./postscript marker, response "
    "language, forbidden words, no commas, repeating the prompt), and if any fail, "
    "fix them.\n"
    "Output ONLY the final compliant response — no commentary, labels, or code "
    "fences. If the candidate already complied, output it verbatim.\n\n"
    "PROMPT:\n{q}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{d}"
)

# Numeric / counting constraints where the model tends to miscount -> use code.
COUNT_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b\d+\b.*?\b(word|words|sentence|sentences|paragraph|paragraphs|section|"
    r"sections|bullet|bullets|highlight|highlight(?:ed)?|placeholder|placeholders|"
    r"time|times|point|points)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
)


def solve(question, call_model):
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-haiku-4-5", system=SYS)).strip()
    if COUNT_RE.search(question):
        r = call_model(
            CODE_AUDIT.format(q=question, d=draft),
            model="claude-haiku-4-5",
            tools=["code_execution"],
            schema=ANSWER_SCHEMA,
        )
    else:
        r = call_model(
            TEXT_AUDIT.format(q=question, d=draft),
            model="claude-haiku-4-5",
            schema=ANSWER_SCHEMA,
        )
    if r.data and r.data.get("answer"):
        return r.data["answer"].strip()
    return draft

results.name: router_sonnet_hard_path
results.description: Difficulty router with a strong hard-path auditor. Haiku drafts; counting-heavy prompts escalate to a SONNET auditor with code execution (most reliable for cases the cheap model miscounts); easy-to-read constraints get a light haiku text self-check. Only genuinely hard prompts hit sonnet, so average cost stays under a flat sonnet audit. Dev: acc 1.0, $0.0174/query.
results.code:
# Difficulty router with a strong hard-path auditor. Haiku drafts. Prompts whose
# constraints need exact counting escalate to a SONNET auditor with code
# execution (the most reliable combination for the cases the cheap model
# miscounts); prompts with easy-to-read constraints get a light haiku text
# self-check. Because only the genuinely hard prompts hit sonnet, average cost
# stays well under a flat sonnet audit.
import re

SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

CODE_AUDIT = (
    "You are a meticulous formatting-constraint auditor with a Python sandbox.\n"
    "Below is a PROMPT with strict verifiable formatting constraints, and a CANDIDATE "
    "response.\n"
    "1. Enumerate EVERY individual constraint in the prompt (exact word/sentence/"
    "paragraph counts and their >=/<=/== relation, all-lowercase/uppercase, capital "
    "frequency, comma ban, forbidden/required keywords and frequency, exact bullet "
    "count, number of *highlighted* sections, ***-split sections, wrapping the whole "
    "answer in double quotes, required first word, exact end phrase, P.S./postscript "
    "marker, response language, N [bracket] placeholders, repeating the prompt, etc.).\n"
    "2. USE THE CODE EXECUTION TOOL to check the CANDIDATE against each constraint "
    "exactly. Count with code; never eyeball.\n"
    "3. If any fail, revise and RE-CHECK with code until all pass simultaneously.\n"
    "4. Output ONLY the final compliant response — no commentary, labels, or code "
    "fences. If the candidate already complied, output it verbatim.\n\n"
    "PROMPT:\n{q}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{d}"
)

TEXT_AUDIT = (
    "You are a meticulous formatting-constraint auditor. Below is a PROMPT with "
    "strict verifiable formatting constraints and a CANDIDATE response.\n"
    "Enumerate EVERY constraint in the prompt, check the CANDIDATE against each one "
    "carefully (all-lowercase/uppercase, wrapping the whole answer in double quotes, "
    "required first word, exact end phrase, P.S./postscript marker, response "
    "language, forbidden words, no commas, repeating the prompt), and if any fail, "
    "fix them.\n"
    "Output ONLY the final compliant response — no commentary, labels, or code "
    "fences. If the candidate already complied, output it verbatim.\n\n"
    "PROMPT:\n{q}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{d}"
)

COUNT_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b\d+\b.*?\b(word|words|sentence|sentences|paragraph|paragraphs|section|"
    r"sections|bullet|bullets|highlight|highlight(?:ed)?|placeholder|placeholders|"
    r"time|times|point|points)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
)


def solve(question, call_model):
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-haiku-4-5", system=SYS)).strip()
    if COUNT_RE.search(question):
        r = call_model(
            CODE_AUDIT.format(q=question, d=draft),
            model="claude-sonnet-5",
            tools=["code_execution"],
            schema=ANSWER_SCHEMA,
        )
    else:
        r = call_model(
            TEXT_AUDIT.format(q=question, d=draft),
            model="claude-haiku-4-5",
            schema=ANSWER_SCHEMA,
        )
    if r.data and r.data.get("answer"):
        return r.data["answer"].strip()
    return draft

results.name: single_haiku_codeexec
results.description: Single haiku call that both writes and self-verifies with code execution: lists every constraint, drafts, then uses the Python sandbox to check counts/lowercase/commas/bullets/wrapping exactly and revises until all pass. One model tier, exact verification instead of unreliable mental counting. Dev: acc 1.0, $0.0178/query.
results.code:
# Single haiku call that BOTH writes and self-verifies with code execution.
# One model tier, one logical call (plus tool turns), so cheaper than a
# draft+audit pipeline while still using exact code checks instead of the
# model's unreliable counting.
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision, "
    "and you have a Python code-execution sandbox.\n"
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting "
    "constraints. Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content "
    "quality is not judged.\n"
    "Process:\n"
    "1. List every individual formatting constraint (exact word/sentence/paragraph "
    "counts and their relation, all-lowercase/uppercase, capital frequency, comma "
    "ban, forbidden/required keywords and frequency, exact bullet count, number of "
    "*highlighted* sections, ***-split sections, wrapping the whole answer in double "
    "quotes, required first word, exact end phrase, P.S. postscript, response "
    "language, N [bracket] placeholders, repeating the prompt, etc.).\n"
    "2. Draft a response.\n"
    "3. USE THE CODE EXECUTION TOOL to verify the draft against each constraint "
    "exactly (count with code; never eyeball). Revise and re-run until ALL pass.\n"
    "4. Emit ONLY the final compliant response — no preamble, explanation, labels, "
    "or code fences."
)


def solve(question, call_model):
    r = call_model(
        question,
        model="claude-haiku-4-5",
        system=SYS,
        tools=["code_execution"],
        schema=ANSWER_SCHEMA,
    )
    if r.data and r.data.get("answer"):
        return r.data["answer"].strip()
    return str(r).strip()

results.description: Two-pass, all haiku. Cheap draft, then a separate haiku auditor with code execution enumerates every constraint and verifies the draft EXACTLY in the Python sandbox (exact counts, lowercase, commas, bullets, wrapping), repairing until all pass. Cross-context auditor plus exact checks reach high compliance with no expensive thinking tokens. Dev: acc 1.0, $0.0216/query.
results.code:
# All-haiku: cheap draft, then haiku self-audit with code execution to verify
# every constraint EXACTLY (counts, lowercase, commas, bullets, wrapping) and
# repair. Code execution replaces the model's unreliable mental counting, so a
# cheap model can reach high compliance without any expensive thinking tokens.
SYS = (
    "You are an expert at following formatting instructions with perfect precision. "
    "The user's message contains one or more strict, verifiable formatting constraints. "
    "Your ONLY goal is to satisfy EVERY constraint exactly; content quality is not judged. "
    "Output NOTHING except the response itself — no preamble, no explanation, no code "
    "fences, no 'Here is'."
)

AUDIT = (
    "You are a meticulous formatting-constraint auditor with a Python sandbox.\n"
    "Below is a PROMPT containing strict, verifiable formatting constraints, and a "
    "CANDIDATE response.\n"
    "1. Enumerate EVERY individual formatting constraint in the prompt (exact word/"
    "sentence/paragraph counts and their >=/<=/== relation, all-lowercase or all-"
    "uppercase, capital-letter frequency, comma ban, forbidden words, required "
    "keywords and their frequency, exact bullet count, exact number of highlighted "
    "*sections*, number of ***-separated sections, wrapping the whole answer in "
    "double quotes, a required first word, a required exact end phrase, a P.S./"
    "postscript marker, response language, N placeholders in [brackets], repeating "
    "the prompt verbatim, etc.).\n"
    "2. USE THE CODE EXECUTION TOOL to check the CANDIDATE against each constraint "
    "exactly — count words with .split(), count commas, test .islower(), count "
    "bullet lines, count '*...*' spans, verify startswith/endswith, etc. Do not "
    "trust mental counting; run code.\n"
    "3. If any constraint fails, revise the response and RE-CHECK it with code until "
    "all pass simultaneously.\n"
    "4. Output ONLY the final compliant response text — no commentary, labels, or "
    "code fences. If the candidate already complied, output it verbatim.\n\n"
    "PROMPT:\n{q}\n\nCANDIDATE:\n{d}"
)


def solve(question, call_model):
    draft = str(call_model(question, model="claude-haiku-4-5", system=SYS)).strip()
    r = call_model(
        AUDIT.format(q=question, d=draft),
        model="claude-haiku-4-5",
        tools=["code_execution"],
        schema=ANSWER_SCHEMA,
    )
    if r.data and r.data.get("answer"):
        return r.data["answer"].strip()
    return draft
